summary: The customer can reliably reproduce repository indexing failures on a large monorepo, and the logs show the request timing out at the API edge while waiting on the indexing job. Because the runbook says repeated timeouts without a known issue should be escalated, this should be treated as a bug.

evidence.note: The customer reports repeated timeouts on the same large monorepo and notes that onboarding is blocked.
The logs include request req_9da21 timing out at the API edge after 181 seconds while the indexing job was pending.
The runbook says to gather the request ID, confirm reproducibility, and escalate when indexing repeatedly times out without a known issue.

customer_reply: Thanks for the detailed report. We are investigating this indexing failure now. In the meantime, please retry once more and, if possible, use a smaller workspace or subset of the repo as a temporary workaround while we review the timeout behavior.

bug_report.title: Index timeout at API edge during large repository indexing
bug_report.body: Customer reports repeatable index timeout behavior on a 4.2 GB monorepo. Request ID: req_9da21. Evidence points to the API edge timing out while waiting on indexing job idx_4421. Impact: onboarding for new engineers is blocked. Please investigate the API edge timeout path and indexing queue behavior.
